session.user: Run `make lint ty` from the repository root and report results. DO NOT fix any errors - just run the commands and report what happened.
session.assistant: I'll run the `make lint ty` command from the repository root and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run make lint ty to check code quality

session.tool_result:
Exit code 2 make: *** [Makefile:13: lint] Error 1
uv run ruff check I001 [*] Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted --> src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py:1:1 | 1 | / import shutil 2 | | from pathlib import Path 3 | | 4 | | import click 5 | | 6 | | from erk.cli.commands.completions import complete_worktree_names 7 | | from erk.cli.commands.navigation_helpers import ( 8 | | check_pending_learn_marker, 9 | | find_assignment_by_worktree_path, 10 | | ) 11 | | from erk.cli.core import ( 12 | | discover_repo_context, 13 | | validate_worktree_name_for_deletion, 14 | | worktree_path_for, 15 | | ) 16 | | from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 17 | | from erk.core.context import ErkContext, create_context, regenerate_context 18 | | from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext, ensure_erk_metadata_dir 19 | | from erk.core.worktree_pool import load_pool_state 20 | | from erk.core.worktree_utils import ( 21 | | find_worktree_containing_path, 22 | | get_worktree_branch, 23 | | )

Found 1 error.
session.assistant:
The `make lint ty` command failed with an exit code of 2. Here are the results:
## ruff check (linting) - FAILED
**Issue found:**
- **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py:1:1` - **Rule**: `I001` (Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted) - **Message**: Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted - **Status**: Fixable with `--fix` option
**Summary**: Found 1 error - 1 fixable with `--fix`
The imports in `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py` are not properly sorted. The file mixes standard library imports (`import shutil`, `from pathlib import Path`) with third-party imports (`import click`) without proper grouping and sorting according to ruff's isort rules.
The `ty` check did not run because the `lint` target (ruff check) failed first.
